name: Memphis in May Dry Rub
recipeInstructions:
I make something called Memphis in May Dry Rub. To create it, mix 4 teaspoons of paprika, 2 teaspoons of salt, 2 teaspoons of onion powder, 2 teaspoons of black pepper, 2 teaspoons of white pepper, and 1 teaspoon of cayenne in a jar. Close the lid and shake well to combine. Transfer this mixture to a bottle and add 2 cups of apple cider vinegar, shaking it again.
If you find it too hot, reduce the amount of cayenne (to 1/4 or 1/2 teaspoon), black pepper to about 1½ teaspoons, and white pepper to about 1 teaspoon. Optionally, add garlic powder, dry mustard, or a small amount of ground cumin for extra flavor. This recipe is perfect for cold-weather cooking enthusiasts who cook outdoors only occasionally.
